text: Self-assembly of discrete [MₙL₂ₙ] coordination cages (n=2–4) made from square-planar metals centre coordinated by flexible, ‘banana-shaped’ bis-monodentate ligands is reviewed. Several cages with diverse host–guest properties ranging from basic, simple, globular structures to more complex analogues, such as knots, are described. Details on how the properties of the ligands and additional stimuli, like light or redox reactions, could influence the volume of the cavity and the uptake properties towards specific guests are highlighted, focusing on allosteric binding and cooperative recognitions, as well as the creation of molecular switchable ‘flasks’.
